title: calamintha acinos subsp. alpina
intro: Calamintha acinos subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Calamint or Alpine Calmintha, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Calamintha acinos subsp. alpina include its ability to relieve digestive issues, such as indigestion and nausea, as well as its potential to soothe respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. Calamintha acinos subsp. alpina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for its medicinal and therapeutic benefits.
